2007-03-01.title: Iran to Join Iraq Talks in Highest Contact With U.S. in 2 Years
2007-03-01.abstract: The agenda of the talks, which will be held March 10 in Baghdad, will be how to rescue Iraq from a civil war.

2007-03-01.title: Italy: U.S. Will Not Hand Over C.I.A. Suspects
2007-03-01.abstract: The United States would refuse any demand to give up 26 Central Intelligence Agency operatives ordered to stand trial in Italy on charges of kidnapping a radical Egyptian cleric there in 2003.

2007-03-01.title: In U.S. Overtures to Foes, Signs of New Pragmatism
2007-03-01.abstract: The White House appears to have recognized that it has tied its own hands by talking only to friends, experts said.

2007-03-01.title: Car Bomb Kills 7 in Wedding Party in Falluja
2007-03-01.abstract: The incident appeared to be part of a mounting battle between insurgents and locals in western Iraq, according to residents.

2007-03-01.title: Low-Cost Antimalaria Pill Available
2007-03-01.abstract: The medicine is the first product of an innovative collaboration between a drug company and a medical charity.

2007-03-02.title: India: Suspected Maoist Land Mine Kills 8
2007-03-02.abstract: Eight people, including six policemen, died in a land mine blast in the Dantewada district of central Chhattisgarh State, stronghold of Maoist rebels. Four of the policemen belong to a special force with expertise in jungle warfare, brought from India’s northeast.

2007-03-02.title: Pressed by U.S., Pakistan Seizes a Taliban Chief
2007-03-02.abstract: Mullah Obaidullah is the most important Taliban leader to be captured since the U.S.-led invasion of Afghanistan.

2007-03-02.title: 7 at a Baghdad Wedding Are Killed by a Car Bomb
2007-03-02.abstract: The attack appeared to be part of a mounting battle in western Iraq between insurgents with ties to Al Qaeda and other tribal groups.

2007-03-02.title: Caribbean to Screen Passports Using Interpol Global Database
2007-03-02.abstract: The islands will be the first region in the world to check passports against a database of lost or stolen travel documents.

2007-03-02.title: How Quality of Intelligence Is Defined
2007-03-02.abstract: “High confidence” generally indicates our judgments are based on high-quality information and/or the nature of the issue makes it possible to render a solid judgment.

2007-03-03.title: Growing Calls in Australia for Terror Suspect’s Return
2007-03-03.abstract: A “Bring David Hicks Home” movement has gained considerable momentum in Australia in the last few months, even among conservative politicians.

2007-03-03.title: Appeals Court Upholds Dismissal of Abuse Suit
2007-03-03.abstract: A German citizen who says he was kidnapped and abused by the Central Intelligence Agency cannot seek redress in court because his lawsuit would expose state secrets, a United States Court of Appeals ruled on Friday.

2007-03-03.title: An Iraqi Tribal Chief Opposes the Jihadists, and Prays
2007-03-03.abstract: Sheik Abdul Sattar Buzaigh al-Rishawi is a leader of a Sunni Arab tribe that is fighting, at least for now, on the side of the Shiite-led Iraqi government and the American military.

2007-03-03.title: Argentina May See Shared Custody of Its Top Job
2007-03-03.abstract: It seems increasingly likely that President Néstor Kirchner will not seek a second term but rather let his wife become the Peronist candidate.

2007-03-03.title: 8 Sentenced in Morocco for Plotting Terror Attacks
2007-03-03.abstract: The eight included a Tunisian man who Moroccan prosecutors said was the leader of an Islamist cell in Milan with ties to North Africa’s largest terrorist organization.

2007-03-04.title: Police and Protesters Clash in St. Petersburg
2007-03-04.abstract: A large and unruly public protest against the government of President Vladimir V. Putin ended with 100 arrests.

2007-03-04.title: Palestinian Leaders Discuss Unity Government
2007-03-04.abstract: Spokesmen from both Fatah and Hamas expressed hopes that the new government would be formed in the coming week.

2007-03-04.title: British Officials Support Barring BBC Report
2007-03-04.abstract: The public service broadcaster said its report related to an e-mail between confidants of Prime Minister Tony Blair.

2007-03-04.title: 16 Civilians Die as U.S. Troops Open Fire in Afghanistan
2007-03-04.abstract: American officials said the civilians were caught in crossfire with militants, but witnesses and some Afghan officials said the troops shot indiscriminately.

2007-03-04.title: China Plans Big Increase in Military Budget
2007-03-04.abstract: China’s military modernization, particularly its drive to develop advanced weaponry, has been raising concerns.

2007-03-05.title: Buddhists Take Part in Firewalking Ritual in Japan
2007-03-05.abstract: Hundreds of people followed priests in the practice, which is done near the beginning of spring in order to purify mind and body. Those assembled also prayed for health and safety.

2007-03-05.title: Palestinian Leaders Try to Resolve Split Over Key Posts
2007-03-05.abstract: Spokesmen from Fatah and Hamas expressed hopes that the new government would be formed this week despite differences.

2007-03-05.title: Suicide Bombing Kills 20 in Baghdad Book Market
2007-03-05.abstract: The car bombing at the Mutanabi Street market, the embodiment of Baghdad’s venerable intellectual history, had symbolic resonance.

2007-03-05.title: Cities Mesh Across Blurry Border, Despite Physical Barrier
2007-03-05.abstract: The border between San Diego and Tijuana, Mexico is a blurry one, no matter what barriers may be going up to keep people from illegally crossing it.

2007-03-05.title: In Guatemala, Officers’ Killings Echo Dirty War
2007-03-05.abstract: Police corruption, lawlessness and drug traffic plague much of Central America, particularly Guatemala.

2007-03-06.title: Price of a Good Name? For Louvre: $520 Million
2007-03-06.abstract: Abu Dhabi agreed today to pay that much to attach the Louvre’s name to a new museum it hopes to open in 2012.

2007-03-06.title: China Says Japanese Leader’s Comments Won’t Affect Ties
2007-03-06.abstract: China’s foreign but he made clear that Japan’s use of sex slaves during World War II would not dampen China’s desire for stronger ties.

2007-03-06.title: Iranian Women Are Arrested After Protests Outside Court
2007-03-06.abstract: Dozens were  protesting outside a court where five were on trial for leading a campaign to gain more legal rights for women.

2007-03-06.title: Breathing Easier as the Battle for Blue Skies Pays Off
2007-03-06.abstract: A decade and a half after Thailand began a battle for better air quality, this erstwhile icon of smog has emerged as a role model for pollution-choked capitals in Asia.

2007-03-06.title: Russia: Inquiry Into Death of Reporter
2007-03-06.abstract: Prosecutors in Moscow said they had begun a criminal investigation into the death last week of a reporter for Kommersant, Ivan Safranov, who covered military affairs for the Moscow newspaper. He was found on the ground beneath the fifth-floor window in his apartment building on Friday evening. Investigators initially said his death was a suicide. His newspaper, in an article yesterday, said suicide was extremely unlikely, raising the possibility that he had been killed. A motive was not clear.

2007-03-07.title: The Other Somalia: An Island of Stability in a Sea of Armed Chaos
2007-03-07.abstract: The northwest area of Somalia, also known as Somaliland, is peaceful and functional, in contrast to the chaotic mess elsewhere.

2007-03-07.title: U.S. Presses North Korea Over Uranium
2007-03-07.abstract: The United States said that North Korea must fully disclose its efforts to produce highly enriched uranium as part of the negotiations now under way.

2007-03-07.title: U.S. Doctor and Daughter Sickened by Poison in Russia
2007-03-07.abstract: The two women were poisoned with thallium, a highly toxic element with a history of use in pesticides and in murder.

2007-03-07.title: Peace Force Is Attacked on Arrival in Somalia
2007-03-07.abstract: Insurgents shelled the airport where African Union peacekeepers had landed and peppered the area with gunfire, setting off battles across the city.

2007-03-07.title: The Town’s Biggest Event Since the Banana Fever Ended
2007-03-07.abstract: Gabriel García Márquez’s 80th birthday was celebrated  in the Colombian town that inspired the setting for “One Hundred Years of Solitude.”

2007-03-08.title: Israelis Arrest 18 Suspects in Raid on Palestinian Site
2007-03-08.abstract: The arrests of 18 men suspected of being militants appears to be part of a growing security clampdown in the West Bank.

2007-03-08.title: Commons Moves to Erode Nobles’ Power in Britain
2007-03-08.abstract: The move to introduce elections to the House of Lords could replace hereditary peers with elected legislators.

2007-03-08.title: Attacks on Shiite Pilgrims Continue
2007-03-08.abstract: The attacks were aimed at reigniting a cycle of sectarian retribution as hundreds of thousands of Shiites made their pilgrimage to Karbala.

2007-03-08.title: Turkey: Court Pulls Plug on YouTube
2007-03-08.abstract: An Istanbul court ordered access to the video sharing Web site YouTubeblocked because of a clip on the site that was said to insult the nation’s revered founder, Mustafa Kemal Ataturk. Critical comments against Ataturk are barred by law. YouTube later said it had removed the clip, and another court ruling said access to the service could be restored. According to news reports, the clip was part of a “virtual war” of insults and name-calling being waged on YouTube between Turks and Greeks.

2007-03-08.title: Buildup in Iraq Needed Into ’08, U.S. General Says
2007-03-08.abstract: The recommendation reflects the military’s new counterinsurgency doctrine, which puts a premium on trying to win over a wary population.

2007-03-09.title: U.N. Hopes Ex-Rebels Will Buy Into Kosovo Peace Deal
2007-03-09.abstract: The U.N. deal gives the Albanian population considerable independence, though with a dose of international supervision.

2007-03-09.title: Bush’s South America Tour Incites Fight for Attention
2007-03-09.abstract: President Bush ended up competing for attention with President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela, who called the American visit an act of imperialism, adding, “Gringo, go home!”

2007-03-09.title: New U.S. Commander in Iraq Won’t Rule Out Need for Added Troops
2007-03-09.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus left open the possibility of calling in even more soldiers as he described the difficult task of calming the country.

2007-03-09.title: Brazilian Politician Indicted in New York in Kickback Scheme
2007-03-09.abstract: New York prosecutors announced the indictment of Paulo Maluf in a construction kickback scheme at the expense of Brazilian taxpayers.

2007-03-09.title: Democrats Rally Behind a Pullout From Iraq in ’08
2007-03-09.abstract: Republicans vowed to block the new Democratic effort, which they said amounted to micromanaging the war.

2007-03-10.title: U.S. Soldiers Accused of Shooting Civilians in Sadr City
2007-03-10.abstract: The American military said it was investigating an episode involving “an escalation of force,” but could not confirm any details of the alleged shooting.

2007-03-10.title: Zimbabwe: Newspaper Shuts Down
2007-03-10.abstract: A newspaper widely said to have been taken over by the nation’s secret service in 2005, The Daily Mirror, has suspended publication because of a lack of newsprint, the paper’s chairman, Ibbo Mandaza, said. The German news agency Deutsche Presse Agentur reported that some journalists at the newspaper have not been paid since the start of the year. Zimbabwe struggles with rampant inflation and shortages of foreign currency, fuel, food and many other commodities.

2007-03-10.title: France: Doctors Petition for Euthanasia
2007-03-10.abstract: More than 2,000 French doctors and nurses have signed a petition admitting that they had helped terminally ill patients die and calling on the government to legalize euthanasia. The petition, which was published Thursday in the newsmagazine Le Nouvel Observateur, comes days before the trial of a doctor and a nurse charged with administering a fatal dose of potassium to a woman suffering from terminal pancreatic cancer in 2003. Actively ending a patient’s life is illegal in France, though withholding treatment with a patient’s consent is allowed under some circumstances.

2007-03-10.title: Democrats Steer the War in Iraq in Fits and Starts
2007-03-10.abstract: House Democrats range from nervous centrists to an antiwar contingent that wants a swift withdrawal.

2007-03-10.title: Government-Rebel Fighting Forces Sri Lankans to Flee
2007-03-10.abstract: COLOMBO, Sri Lanka, March 10 — Thousands of civilians fled into government-controlled territory today and on Friday as the military started a fresh offensive to capture rebel held areas in eastern Sri Lanka.

2007-03-11.title: Palestinian Convoy Attacked
2007-03-11.abstract: Unidentified gunmen fired at the convoy of a cabinet minister from the Islamist movement Hamas, wounding one of his escorts.

2007-03-11.title: U.S. and Iran See Iraq Talks as Good ‘First Step’
2007-03-11.abstract: WASHINGTON, March 11 — Both American and Iranian diplomats today cautiously welcomed as an important “first step” the outcome of a regional meeting on the violence in Iraq, which brought a rare face-to-face encounter between the two sides.

2007-03-11.title: Dismay Over New U.N. Human Rights Council
2007-03-11.abstract: Critics say the new council is not a sufficient improvement over the former, widely discredited, Human Rights Commission.

2007-03-11.title: Palestinian Official’s Convoy Is Attacked in the West Bank
2007-03-11.abstract: JERUSALEM, March 10 — Unidentified gunmen fired at the convoy of a Palestinian cabinet minister from the Islamist movement Hamas in the West Bank today, wounding one of his escorts. Hamas condemned the attack and said its political rival Fatah was responsible, according to Palestinian news reports.

2007-03-11.title: Palestinian and Israeli Leaders Meet Again
2007-03-11.abstract: After the leaders’ second meeting in a month, aides said that there was little concrete progress to report.

2007-03-12.title: Arbaeen in Karbala
2007-03-12.abstract: Millions of Shiites made a pilgrimage to Karbala, Iraq, despite the threat of snipers and suicide bombers.

2007-03-12.title: Iran’s President Wants to Tell Security Council of Nuclear Aims
2007-03-12.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad wants to take part in a U.N. Security Council meeting to brief member states about Iran’s nuclear program.

2007-03-12.title: Israeli Officials Praise Saudi Peace Initiative
2007-03-12.abstract: Officials spoke of “positive” elements in the Saudi proposal, which was dismissed when it was offered in 2002.

2007-03-12.title: In New Tactic, Militants Burn Houses in Iraq
2007-03-12.abstract: Residents in Diyala Province said the attackers sought to scare people into giving in to extremism or running away.

2007-03-12.title: New Defense Chief Eases Relations Rumsfeld Bruised
2007-03-12.abstract: Robert M. Gates is reviewing the Pentagon’s dealings with the nation’s spy agencies to heal working relationships bruised by his predecessor.

2007-03-13.title: For U.S. Troops at War, Liquor Is Spur to Crime
2007-03-13.abstract: Strictly forbidden by the U.S. military in Iraq and Afghanistan, alcohol is a factor in a growing number of crimes by troops.

2007-03-13.title: Bush Meets Anger Over Immigration Issue as He Promotes Free Trade in Guatemala
2007-03-13.abstract: During his visit to the struggling Central American nation, President Bush was  confronted with an angry, outside-in perspective on the immigration debate raging at home.

2007-03-13.title: Companion of Bomber in Casablanca Is Questioned
2007-03-13.abstract: A man who had been sitting with the suicide attacker was caught while trying to flee, then found to have explosives concealed on his body as well.

2007-03-13.title: Canada: Manslaughter Charge in Afghanistan Case
2007-03-13.abstract: A soldier was charged with manslaughter in the shooting death of a fellow soldier in Afghanistan. Master Cpl. Jeffrey Walsh was killed Aug. 9 when a gun discharged during a patrol outside Kandahar in southern Afghanistan. Master Cpl. Robbie Fraser was charged with one count of manslaughter in the case and one count of negligent performance of duty. Master Corporal Fraser has returned to his base in Shilo, Manitoba. Both were in Afghanistan as members of the Princess Patricia’s Canadian Light Infantry.

2007-03-13.title: French Premier Throws Support for Presidency to Political Rival
2007-03-13.abstract: Prime Minister Dominique de Villepin formally endorsed his political rival, the center-right candidate Nicolas Sarkozy, for president.

2007-03-14.title: Treasury Reportedly Set to Act to Free North Korean Money
2007-03-14.abstract: American officials see the step by the Treasury as a crucial part of the recent deal to disarm North Korea’s nuclear program.

2007-03-14.title: Britain Drafts Laws to Slash Carbon Emissions
2007-03-14.abstract: Some of the measures in the plan could be unpopular with voters even as they inspire politicians’ acclaim.

2007-03-14.title: Ecuador: Lawmakers and Police Clash in Congress
2007-03-14.abstract: A group of fired legislators clashed with the police after forcing their way into Congress as President Rafael Correa’s plan to rewrite the Constitution continued to generate protests. Dozens of protesters supporting Mr. Correa also gathered in front of the building, screaming epithets to the group of about 20 legislators, who were dismissed last week by the Electoral Tribunal after trying to reverse a ruling that could have potentially eroded their power. Mr. Correa, who is seeking to diminish the easy ability of Congress to oust presidents in Ecuador, accused the fired legislators of “abusing the misery of the people.” Mr. Correa is Ecuador’s eighth president in 10 years.

2007-03-14.title: Whaling: A Japanese Obsession With American Roots
2007-03-14.abstract: For Japan, whaling is intricately tied to the country’s relations with the West, especially the United States.

2007-03-14.title: Answering Latin Left, Bush Pledges to Help Poor
2007-03-14.abstract: The president’s Latin American tour represented an attempt to stave off growing anti-American sentiment.

2007-03-15.title: Sunni Who Gave Comfort in Dark Night of War Pays the Price
2007-03-15.abstract: In Sunni Arab neighborhoods, even Sunnis helping fellow Sunnis are viewed as traitors to the insurgent cause.

2007-03-15.title: U.S. Troops to Open 100 Garrisons Across Baghdad
2007-03-15.abstract: The increase in the number of outposts is part of the plan to put American troops back on the streets.

2007-03-15.title: Dissident Priest in El Salvador Is Rebuked by the Vatican
2007-03-15.abstract: The Vatican said the writings of a Jesuit priest well known in Latin America were “either erroneous or dangerous.”

2007-03-15.title: 50 Protesters in Zimbabwe Are Set Free Without Charges
2007-03-15.abstract: An unknown number remain hospitalized after riot police set upon a group at a prayer meeting on Sunday.

2007-03-15.title: Britain: Lords Reject Elected Chamber
2007-03-15.abstract: After a two-day debate, the House of Lords rebuffed demands to make it an elected body, setting the stage for a fight over the future of the upper chamber. Peers, as members of the Lords are known, voted by 361 to 121 in favor of having all members appointed. Last week, the House of Commons voted by a majority of 113 for a wholly elected Lords and to remove those members who inherit their seats. The gulf between the two houses raises the prospect of months of wrangling as the government searches for a compromise. Pressure to reform the Lords has gathered momentum as the police investigate allegations that the Labor Party offered seats in return for loans and donations.

2007-03-16.title: Attack on Sadr City Mayor Hinders Antimilitia Effort
2007-03-16.abstract: The ambush wounded the mayor and complicated U.S. efforts to rein in a powerful Shiite militia in Sadr City.

2007-03-16.title: U.N. Council Gets New Draft Decree on Iran Nuclear Sanctions
2007-03-16.abstract: The measure expands a roster from an earlier resolution that lists companies and individuals in Iran subject to an assets freeze.

2007-03-16.title: Gunmen in Gaza Fire at Car of U.N. Official
2007-03-16.abstract: No one was hurt in the apparent kidnapping attempt on the Gaza director of the United Nations refugee agency.

2007-03-16.title: Call for Speed Limit Has German Blood at 178 M.P.H. Boil
2007-03-16.abstract: Germany’s love of speed on the autobahn is colliding with fears about global warming, as its drivers spew tons of carbon dioxide into the air.

2007-03-16.title: Corruption Charges Keep Opposition Figure Off Nigeria Ballot
2007-03-16.abstract: The current vice president dismissed the charges as politically motivated and says he is going fight to get his name on the ballot.

2007-03-17.title: Palestinians’ New Unity Can Be Just a Beginning
2007-03-17.abstract: A new strategy to get around the new Palestinian political detente appears to be emerging — one that falls back on elements of Saudi Arabia’s five-year-old peace proposals.

2007-03-17.title: Swelling Crowds Across Pakistan Denounce Musharraf’s Suspension of Chief Justice
2007-03-17.abstract: Riot police officers and protesters clashed and the political opposition joined forces with lawyers who have been rallying for days.

2007-03-17.title: 10 Detainees Pull Off Escape as Visitors Take Their Place
2007-03-17.abstract: Ten inmates in a British military prison in Iraq switched places with visitors as part of an audacious escape plan.

2007-03-17.title: Attacks Kill 2 Iraqis and Expose Hundreds to Chlorine Gas
2007-03-17.abstract: Three suicide attacks in Anbar Province employed a tactic that insurgents began using earlier this year, the U.S. military said.

2007-03-17.title: Russia: Return Dacha, Court Tells Ex-Premier
2007-03-17.abstract: A court in Moscow has for the second time ordered Mikhail M. Kasyanov, a former prime minister, to return a luxury home he bought from the state in 2004, ruling that he had acquired it illegally. Mr. Kasyanov, who served under President Vladimir V. Putin from 2000 to 2004, dismissed the decision as politically motivated and vowed to appeal, as he did when a court ordered the return a year ago. The purchase came under scrutiny after he declared his opposition to Mr. Putin and vowed to run for president next year.

2007-03-18.title: Palestinian Lawmakers Ratify Unity Government
2007-03-18.abstract: Despite the vote, there were cracks visible between Hamas and Fatah, including over whether to renounce violence.

2007-03-18.title: U.S. and Israel Differ on Palestinian Contacts
2007-03-18.abstract: The first fractures surfaced in the Israeli and American approaches to the new Palestinian unity government.

2007-03-18.title: Suicide Bombers Using Chlorine Gas Kill 2 and Sicken Hundreds in Western Iraq
2007-03-18.abstract: Officials say insurgents began combining explosives with chlorine gas and other chemicals this year in an effort to sow more fear and havoc among Iraqi civilians.

2007-03-18.title: Sudanese in Israel Hope They Have Found a Home
2007-03-18.abstract: Israel has become the unlikely destination for African refugees, many of whom are fleeing the violence in Darfur.

2007-03-18.title: Abu Dhabi Explores Energy Alternatives
2007-03-18.abstract: The capital of an oil-rich land is embracing clean-energy technology using the sun, wind and hydrogen.

2007-03-19.title: Renewed Interest in Japanese Who Died in Epic Battle
2007-03-19.abstract: A sudden surge of interest in the Battle of Iwo Jima is forcing Japan to remember a war that it lost, and those who died in it.

2007-03-19.title: Detainee Alleges He Was Beaten in U.S. Custody
2007-03-19.abstract: In an affidavit, David Hicks contends that he was beaten during interrogations and saw abuse of other prisoners.

2007-03-19.title: Opposition Official Beaten as Zimbabwe Crackdown Grows
2007-03-19.abstract: Foreign travel by leading government critics was blocked and an official of the main opposition party was severely beaten.

2007-03-19.title: Iraq Says Raid on Sunni Lawmaker’s Home Yielded Arms Cache and Cars With TNT Traces
2007-03-19.abstract: Iraqi officials gave no explanation of what led to the raid, the most significant one against a Parliament member in months.

2007-03-19.title: U.S. and North Korea End Frozen-Funds Impasse
2007-03-19.abstract: The agreement on frozen funds clears the way for talks to focus on implementing a nuclear disarmament accord.

2007-03-20.title: Hamas Militants Take Credit for Sniper Attack
2007-03-20.abstract: An Israeli worker for an electric utility company was wounded by a sniper near the border with the Gaza Strip today, and the armed wing of Hamas claimed responsibility.

2007-03-20.title: Sniper Wounds a Utility Worker in Israel
2007-03-20.abstract: The armed wing of Hamas claimed responsibility for the attack, its first since a shaky cease-fire came into effect last November.

2007-03-20.title: Lebanese Army Tightens Security at Camp to Press Militant Group
2007-03-20.abstract: The Lebanese Army maintained a heavy presence on Monday in a Palestinian refugee camp where an Islamic militant group is based.

2007-03-20.title: Russia Objects to U.N. Plan for Kosovo as ‘One-Sided’
2007-03-20.abstract: Russia signaled its opposition to the United Nations proposal to settle the status of Kosovo and said a new negotiator should be appointed and fresh talks started.

2007-03-20.title: Don’t ‘Pack Up,’ Bush Says After 4 Years of War
2007-03-20.abstract: President Bush marked the fourth anniversary of the invasion of Iraq by warning that hard days lie ahead.

2007-03-21.title: A Journey That Will Come Full Circle and End With a Ring
2007-03-21.abstract: The bells of Lowell House at Harvard will soon return to the Russian monastery from which they were sold more than 70 years ago.

2007-03-21.title: Italy Swapped 5 Jailed Taliban for a Hostage
2007-03-21.abstract: It appears to be the first time prisoners have been openly exchanged for a hostage in the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq.

2007-03-21.title: Sensing Shift in Bush Policy, Another Hawk Leaves
2007-03-21.abstract: Robert Joseph is the latest to leave an administration that some conservatives say has lost its clarity of mission.

2007-03-21.title: Britain Proposes Allowing Schools to Forbid Full-Face Muslim Veils
2007-03-21.abstract: The recommendation was the latest episode in a saga of rancorous discussion of the full-face veil, known as the niqab.

2007-03-21.title: Crowds in Somalia Mutilate Bodies of Soldiers
2007-03-21.abstract: Full-scale fighting broke out in Mogadishu and furious crowds mutilated the bodies of government soldiers, chanting, “We will burn you alive!”

2007-03-22.title: Fighting Between Tribes and Outside Militants Grows in Largely Lawless Region of Pakistan
2007-03-22.abstract: At least 110 people have been killed since Monday in the tribal area of South Waziristan, government and security officials said.

2007-03-22.title: Where the Wrong Gift Can Mean Love’s Labor’s Lost
2007-03-22.abstract: A celebrity divorce shows that as South Korea’s wealth grows, choosing the right wedding gifts for the new in-laws is becoming fraught with pitfalls.

2007-03-22.title: Germany Cites Koran in Rejecting Divorce
2007-03-22.abstract: A judge turned down a German Muslim woman’s request for a fast-track divorce on the ground that her husband beat her.

2007-03-22.title: France: Education Official Fired for Opposing Muslim School
2007-03-22.abstract: France replaced a regional school board president, Alain Morvan, for vigorously opposing a new Muslim high school.

2007-03-22.title: University in Moscow to Investigate Student Claims
2007-03-22.abstract: In a rare burst of defiance and activism, students have made accusations that teaching standards and living conditions have been severely eroded at one of Russia’s most prestigious universities.

2007-03-23.title: Iran Detains British Sailors in Waters Off Iraq
2007-03-23.abstract: Fifteen British sailors were captured at gunpoint in the Persian Gulf and taken into custody by Iranian forces today, the British Ministry of Defense said.

2007-03-23.title: Italy: No Regrets Over Taliban Deal
2007-03-23.abstract: Foreign Minister Massimo D’Alema said he did not discuss with American officials the deal in which five Taliban prisoners were traded this week for the freedom of a kidnapped Italian reporter in Afghanistan. Despite strong criticism from officials in the United States and Europe, Mr. D’Alema told reporters in Rome, “I am not the slightest bit repentant for having saved” the reporter, Daniele Mastrogiacomo, adding that it “is preferable to have a controversy over having saved him, rather than for having had him killed.”.

2007-03-23.title: German Judge Cites Koran, Stirring Up Cultural Storm
2007-03-23.abstract: A judge turned down a German Muslim woman’s request for a fast-track divorce on the ground that her husband beat her.

2007-03-23.title: Israeli Soldiers Stand Firm, but Duty Wears on the Soul
2007-03-23.abstract: Tense checkpoints and an inconclusive war have fed a mood of introspection, anxiety and gloominess in Israel.

2007-03-23.title: French Court Rules for Newspaper That Printed Muhammad Cartoons
2007-03-23.abstract: The paper and its editor were accused by two Muslim groups of “publicly abusing a group of people because of their religion.”

2007-03-24.title: After Congo Elections
2007-03-24.abstract: Six months after a historic democratic election, will Congo find stability? Jeffrey Gettleman reports from Goma.

2007-03-24.title: Russia: Detentions Reported Ahead of Anti-Kremlin March
2007-03-24.abstract: In advance of an anti-Kremlin protest march set for today in Nizhny Novgorod, Russia’s third-largest city after Moscow and St. Petersburg, the police detained several planners and confiscated 60,000 copies of an opposition newspaper, organizers of the protest, called the Dissenters’ March, said. One protest planner, Oksana Chlysheva of the local Foundation to Support Tolerance, said that the police and other security officers had begun to mass in the city and that unidentified people have visited schools, universities and stores warning people not to attend.

2007-03-24.title: Suspicions Turn to Cricket’s Dark Side
2007-03-24.abstract: The strangling death of the coach of Pakistan’s national cricket team has gained the world’s attention.

2007-03-24.title: Plane Is Hit in Somali Capital as Threat to Government Builds
2007-03-24.abstract: The incident is raising fears that Somalia’s transitional government faces an increasingly organized and lethal threat.

2007-03-24.title: Britain Questions 2005 Bombing Suspects
2007-03-24.abstract: The British police began interrogating three men  for alleged offenses related to the London suicide bombings of July 7, 2005.

2007-03-25.title: Hong Kong Leader Is Re-elected
2007-03-25.abstract: Donald Tsang won the first contested election for the territory’s top job since it was returned to China in 1997.

2007-03-25.title: Witnesses Missing in Coach’s Death
2007-03-25.abstract: The police investigation was complicated by the fact that many potential witnesses had already left Jamaica.

2007-03-25.title: Marking Union’s 50th, Europe Is Anxious on Future
2007-03-25.abstract: Festivities were shadowed by a sense that the European Union has a divided present and an uncertain path ahead.

2007-03-25.title: C.I.A. Awaits Rules on Terrorism Interrogations
2007-03-25.abstract: The agency has been left without authority to use harsh techniques that the White House said were necessary.

2007-03-25.title: Russian Police Trample Anti-Putin Protest
2007-03-25.abstract: Riot police officers swarmed on a group of several dozen journalists and demonstrators, cutting off a protest against the government of President Vladimir V. Putin.

2007-03-26.title: European Union, at 50, Seeks Footing as the World Shifts
2007-03-26.abstract: Festivities were shadowed by a sense that the European Union has a divided present and an uncertain path ahead.

2007-03-26.title: Rice Aims for Delicate Balance in Mideast Shuttle
2007-03-26.abstract: The secretary of state prodded the Israeli and Palestinian leaders to agree, at least, to negotiating objectives.

2007-03-26.title: India Attracts Universities From the U.S.
2007-03-26.abstract: American universities, eager to expand to markets abroad, are training their sights on India.

2007-03-26.title: Pakistan Detains Opposition Members
2007-03-26.abstract: The government said claims that hundreds of political opposition members have been detained were exaggerated.

2007-03-26.title: Iraq Moves to Ease Restraints on Baath Party Members
2007-03-26.abstract: Officials plan to introduce modifications to a law that restricts former Baathists  from holding government jobs.

2007-03-27.title: Suicide Attack on Sri Lankan Army Base Kills 7
2007-03-27.abstract: A day after guerrillas bombed a military air base, a pair of suspected rebel suicide bombers tried to drive a tractor loaded with explosives into an army camp in the country’s east.

2007-03-27.title: Republicans to Rely on President Bush’s Veto to Block Troop Withdrawal Plan
2007-03-27.abstract: As the Senate opened debate on a $122 billion Iraq spending bill, Republicans vowed not to allow Congress to impose a withdrawal date for American troops.

2007-03-27.title: From Ashes of Yukos, New Russian Oil Giant Emerges
2007-03-27.abstract: On Tuesday, the remaining assets of what was once Russia’s richest company will be auctioned, signaling the final stage for Yukos.

2007-03-27.title: Rice Works to Bring Israel’s Olmert and Arab Leaders Closer on What Issues Can Be Broached
2007-03-27.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice pressed Israeli Prime Minister Ehud Olmert to agree to peace talks that would include three crucual issues.

2007-03-27.title: Foregone Conclusion Appears to Keep Egyptian Voters Home
2007-03-27.abstract: By government reports, the turnout in a referendum on 34 constitutional amendments was high, but in reality many polling stations were largely empty.

2007-03-28.title: Tajik President Outlaws Slavic Endings on Names
2007-03-28.abstract: The unusual decree is one of several that the  leader implemented in an effort to purge vestiges of the former Soviet rule.

2007-03-28.title: Typical French Town Is Split Over Election
2007-03-28.abstract: With the first round of the presidential election looming, almost half the voters in France’s “average town” are undecided.

2007-03-28.title: Thai Coup Leader Calls for Emergency Powers
2007-03-28.abstract: The general asked his appointed prime minister to impose a state of emergency in Bangkok as opposition to the government grew.

2007-03-28.title: Charges of Vote Rigging as Egypt Approves Constitution Changes
2007-03-28.abstract: The disputed measures empower the Egyptian president to dissolve Parliament, to suspend civil protections and to limit judges’ oversight of elections.

2007-03-28.title: Russia: Putin Chum Picked as Elections Chief
2007-03-28.abstract: The central election committee, dominated by supporters of President Vladimir V. Putin, elected as its chairman a former colleague of Mr. Putin’s from St. Petersburg, Vladimir V. Churov. Mr. Churov, a member of the lower house of Parliament since 2003, worked with Mr. Putin in the St. Petersburg governor’s office in the 1990s. He replaces Aleksandr A. Veshnyakov, whom Mr. Putin ousted this month after he criticized new election laws. Russia is approaching elections for Parliament and president.

2007-03-29.title: Ecuador: Court Renews Power Struggle
2007-03-29.abstract: Congress was in turmoil after Jorge Cevallos, its elected leader, moved to void an effort to expel 57 opposition legislators for trying to block President Rafael Correa’s plan to rewrite the Constitution to make it harder for Congress to topple presidents.

2007-03-29.title: U.S. Iraq Role Is Called Illegal by Saudi King
2007-03-29.abstract: King Abdullah’s speech at a meeting of Arab leaders underscored growing differences between Saudi Arabia and the Bush administration.

2007-03-29.title: For Some G.I.’s, Less Time at Home, More in Iraq
2007-03-29.abstract: Army combat troops are likely to have to go back to Iraq with less than a year at home if the Bush administration decides to maintain higher force levels, a general said.

2007-03-29.title: Iran Takes Harder Line in Dispute Over Britons
2007-03-29.abstract: Iran insisted that Britain admit fault before any of the 15 captured British sailors and marines are released.

2007-03-29.title: Transcript of Seized Sailor’s Statement
2007-03-29.abstract: Following is a transcript of a statement by Faye Turney, a captured British sailor, that was broadcast on Iranian state television:.

2007-03-30.title: Somalia Battle Leaves 30 Dead and Shreds Hopes for Peace
2007-03-30.abstract: Ethiopian troops stormed into the center of the capital, Mogadishu, setting off clashes that killed more than 30 people.

2007-03-30.title: Iraqi Widow Saves Her Home, but Victory Is Brief
2007-03-30.abstract: The final hours of Suaada Saadoun’s life reveal the fierce pressure Sunnis face from Shiite militias in Baghdad.

2007-03-30.title: Heads of Arab States Prod Israel to Embrace Peace Offer
2007-03-30.abstract: The leaders of 21 Arab governments called on Israel to withdraw from the land it occupied in the 1967 war in exchange for full diplomatic relations with them.

2007-03-30.title: Pressed, Kyrgyz President Names His Critic as Premier
2007-03-30.abstract: The appointment provides a highly visible platform for an opposition figure in a region uniformly dominated by one-party politics.

2007-03-30.title: Defying Bush, Senate Passes Iraq Spending Measure
2007-03-30.abstract: The bill provided more than $97.5 billion for military operations in Iraq and Afghanistan but ordered troop withdrawals from Iraq to begin within 120 days.

2007-03-31.title: Supporter of Putin Proposes, Once Again, to Extend Russian Leader’s Term Beyond Next Year
2007-03-31.abstract: A proposal amending Russia’s Constitution to allow Vladimir V. Putin to extend his presidency beyond its legal end in 2008 seemed to gain new life on Friday.

2007-03-31.title: Army’s War Funds Can Last Through July, Report Says
2007-03-31.abstract: Democrats quickly seized on the findings to defend their handling of the legislation to pay for military operations in Iraq and Afghanistan.

2007-03-31.title: In Japan, a Historian Stands by Proof of Wartime Sex Slavery
2007-03-31.abstract: Yoshiaki Yoshimi found a rare trove of official documents that uncovered the Japanese military’s direct role in managing brothels during World War II.

2007-03-31.title: Violence in Somalia Continues
2007-03-31.abstract: Somali rebels shot down an Ethiopian helicopter and fired rocket-propelled grenades at Ethiopian troops in Mogadishu.

2007-03-31.title: French Nun Says Church to Rule on Cure by John Paul II
2007-03-31.abstract: The Vatican’s decision on the veracity of a French nun’s story of being cured of Parkinson’s disease is crucial to making Pope John Paul II a saint.
